mechanism: Empirical hypothesis testing through multi-condition experimental designs where behavioral or affective manipulations are crossed with dependent measures of cognitive performance. Participants receive priming or induction of affect-congruent states (positive/negative), then perform memory or judgment tasks while their performance is recorded and analyzed via ANOVA and mediation analysis.

Between-subjects factorial experiments manipulating categorical independent variables (processing style: global/local, primes: positive/negative/neutral, standards: high/low) and measuring dependent variables through ANOVAs, planned contrasts, and mediation analyses to test interaction effects on judgment outcomes.

Controlled laboratory negotiation task with information asymmetry manipulations and emotion signaling. Allocators distribute chips under different information conditions (symmetric/asymmetric) after receiving opponent emotion cues. Offers and perceived limits are measured; perceived limits mediate effects via regression analysis.

Longitudinal dyadic and cross-sectional studies with path analysis and mediation testing. Manipulations of felt responsiveness are implemented; perceptions of partner responsiveness and relationship-promotion outcomes are measured. Effects are tested using SAS CALIS path analysis and Sobel mediation tests.

Experimental manipulations of perceived personal control via memory recall tasks. Control manipulation is crossed with measurements of beliefs about external systems (God, government). Outcomes are analyzed via univariate ANOVA with moderators, mediation via bootstrapping, and multilevel modeling for cross-national data.

Multi-study experimental designs where participants are assigned to engage in or observe morally problematic behavior (speech writing, racist task), then rate confederates who comply or rebel. Outcomes measured via ANOVAs on attraction indices, factor analyses for agency/communion, mediation via Sobel tests and self-affirmation manipulation.
